task.name: Stroop Online
reference_literature: Stroop, J. R. (1935). Studies of interference in serial verbal reactions. JEP.
MacLeod, C. M. (1991). Half a century of research on the Stroop effect. Psych Bull.
Hedge, C., Powell, G., & Sumner, P. (2018). The reliability paradox. Behav Res Methods.

stimuli.description: A color word rendered in red ink

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if (!el) return false; const c = el.firstElementChild; if (!c) return false; const style = (c.getAttribute('style')||'').toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g,''); return style.includes('color:red'); })()

response.response_key_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if (!el) return null; const c = el.firstElementChild; const style = (c?.getAttribute('style')||'').toLowerCase(); const m = style.match(/color:\s*(\w+)/); return m ? m[1][0] : null; })()

response.response_key_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); const c = el?.firstElementChild; const style = (c?.getAttribute('style')||'').toLowerCase(); const m = style.match(/color:\s*(\w+)/); return m ? m[1][0] : null; })()

task_specific.notes: Stimulus condition (congruent vs incongruent) is determined by whether the displayed word text matches the ink color. The response is always the first letter of the ink color, regardless of condition. Since both conditions produce the same stimulus markup (a word in a color), we tag all ink colors with condition 'congruent' as a placeholder and override via response_key_js. The true per-trial condition is determined at analysis time from the word text vs ink color.

autocorrelation.rationale: Modest lag-1 RT autocorrelation is commonly observed in speeded choice tasks (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004).

post_error_slowing.rationale: Post-error slowing is robustly observed in Stroop tasks (Rabbitt, 1966; Danielmeier & Ullsperger, 2011).

condition_repetition.rationale: Conflict adaptation / Gratton effect produces small sequential condition effects (Gratton et al., 1992).

pink_noise.rationale: 1/f noise is characteristic of human RT series (Gilden, 2001).

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Individual differences in baseline RT are substantial (~80 ms SD) in online Stroop samples; Stroop effect magnitude varies less.

pilot.rationale: 15-trial task; 8 trials sufficient to verify detection across all four ink colors. Only 'congruent' is used as the placeholder condition label since all stimuli share the same markup.

phase_detection.instructions: !!document.querySelector('.jspsych-instructions-nav') || (document.body && document.body.innerText && (document.body.innerText.includes('Press Space to continue') || document.body.innerText.includes('Press Space to start')))

timing.trial_context_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if (!el) return ''; const c = el.firstElementChild; if (!c) return ''; const text = (c.textContent||'').trim(); const style = (c.getAttribute('style')||'').toLowerCase(); const m = style.match(/color:\s*(\w+)/); const ink = m ? m[1] : ''; return 'word='+text+';ink='+ink+';congruent='+(text.toLowerCase()===ink.toLowerCase()); })()
